skill.name: teach
skill.description: Teach the user a new skill or concept, within this workspace.
skill.summary: A stateful teaching skill that guides users through structured lessons, learning records, and mission-driven knowledge acquisition.
skill.author: mattpocock

security_audit.summary: This skill consists entirely of markdown documentation describing a teaching workflow. The static analyzer reported 45 potential issues, but all are false positives. The flagged 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' patterns are markdown code fences (triple backticks) in format templates, not executable code. 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' flags are false positives triggered by harmless text patterns. 'System/Network reconnaissance' flags refer to scanning a local directory for file naming, not network activity. 'Hardcoded URLs' are placeholder examples in documentation. No executable code, no scripts, no real network calls exist in this skill.

low_findings.title: Static scanner false positives on markdown content
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged 45 potential security issues, but all are false positives. The skill contains only markdown documentation (SKILL.md, GLOSSARY-FORMAT.md, LEARNING-RECORD-FORMAT.md, MISSION-FORMAT.md, RESOURCES-FORMAT.md) with no executable code. 'Backtick execution' flags are markdown code fences, 'crypto algorithm' flags are text patterns, 'reconnaissance' flags refer to directory scanning for file naming, and 'hardcoded URLs' are placeholder examples in templates.

content.user_title: Teach yourself anything with structured AI-guided lessons
content.value_statement: Learning a new topic is hard when knowledge is scattered and progress is invisible. This skill creates a stateful teaching workspace that grounds every lesson in your mission and tracks what you have learned.
actual_capabilities: Creates a mission document that captures why you want to learn a topic
Generates self-contained HTML lessons saved to a lessons directory
Maintains learning records that track your progress and prior knowledge
Curates a resources list of trusted knowledge sources and communities
Builds a glossary that defines terms used across all lessons
Adapts teaching to your zone of proximal development based on prior records
limitations: Requires user initiative to provide the initial topic and mission
Lesson quality depends on the quality of resources in RESOURCES.md
Does not replace real-world practice or community interaction
Works best when the user provides feedback on each lesson

use_cases.title: Learning a new programming language
use_cases.description: Set up a workspace to learn Rust, Python, or any language with mission-driven lessons and progress tracking.

use_cases.title: Studying a physical skill like yoga
use_cases.description: Create a mission for yoga practice with pose references, progressive lessons, and community resources.

use_cases.title: Researching a complex domain
use_cases.description: Build a knowledge base around a topic like machine learning or history with curated resources and structured lessons.

prompt_templates.title: Start a new learning topic
prompt_templates.prompt: I want to learn about {topic}. Help me set up a teaching workspace and write a MISSION.md.

prompt_templates.title: Create a lesson on a specific concept
prompt_templates.prompt: Based on my current learning records, create the next lesson on {concept} that fits my zone of proximal development.

prompt_templates.title: Add resources to the workspace
prompt_templates.prompt: Find high-quality resources about {topic} and add them to RESOURCES.md with annotations.

prompt_templates.title: Record what was learned
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a learning record for what I just demonstrated about {concept}, including evidence and implications.

output_examples.input: I want to learn about TypeScript generics.
output: Creates MISSION.md with your learning goal and success criteria
Scans for existing learning records to determine your level
Designs a lesson on TypeScript generics in your zone of proximal development
Saves the lesson as a self-contained HTML file in ./lessons/

output_examples.input: Add a resource about functional programming.
output: Identifies a high-quality resource on functional programming
Adds it to RESOURCES.md under the Knowledge section
Annotates the entry with what it covers and when to use it

output_examples.input: I already know how to use map and filter in JavaScript.
output: Creates a learning record noting your prior knowledge
Records the depth of your understanding
Adjusts future lessons to skip basic array methods

best_practices: Always populate MISSION.md before starting lessons to ground teaching in real goals
Wait for evidence of understanding before adding terms to the glossary
Review and prune RESOURCES.md regularly to keep it high-signal
Write learning records only for non-obvious insights, not routine coverage
anti_patterns: Do not teach without a populated MISSION.md
Do not add glossary terms the user has not yet demonstrated understanding of
Do not trust parametric knowledge over curated resources in RESOURCES.md

faq.question: What files does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates MISSION.md, RESOURCES.md, NOTES.md, a lessons/ directory, a learning-records/ directory, and a reference/ directory.

faq.question: How does it track my progress?
faq.answer: It writes learning records in ./learning-records/ each time you demonstrate understanding of a concept.

faq.question: Can I use it for physical skills like yoga?
faq.answer: Yes. It works for any topic. The philosophy separates knowledge, skills, and wisdom.

faq.question: How does it decide what to teach next?
faq.answer: It reads your learning records and mission to find your zone of proximal development.

faq.question: Does it need internet access?
faq.answer: No. It works with local markdown files. External resources are listed but not fetched automatically.

faq.question: What format are lessons saved in?
faq.answer: Lessons are saved as self-contained HTML files in ./lessons/ with sequential numbering.
